message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Review request: both CSS fixes ready">
Lea has completed both fixes and all tests are green (97 Rust tests + 5 Playwright tests). Please review the changes now:

**Bug 1 fix**: `templates/index.html` — added `class="visually-hidden"` to nav links
**Bug 2 fix**: `static/css/main.css` — added `color: var(--color-fg)` to `.todo-item__toggle`
**Tests**: `e2e/user-journey.spec.ts` — 2 new regression tests + updated existing test

Please read the changed files, review from your area of expertise, and send your approval or feedback. We need consensus from all 8 reviewers before committing.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me review the current state of all changed files.
Good. I've reviewed all three changed files. Let me also quickly verify the integration tests still cover the index route adequately.
I've completed my review. Let me send my approval to the team lead.

input.summary: Approval: both fixes look correct from Rust/backend perspective
input.content:
I have reviewed all three changed files from the Rust engineer perspective. My approval:

**Bug 1 — `templates/index.html` (nav links visibility)**
The fix is correct. Adding `class="visually-hidden"` to both nav links keeps them in the DOM for screen readers while hiding them visually. The Askama template renders cleanly — no Rust handler changes needed since the `IndexTemplate` struct has no fields that changed.

**Bug 2 — `static/css/main.css` (toggle checkbox color)**
Adding `color: var(--color-fg)` to `.todo-item__toggle` at line 772 correctly overrides the inherited `color: white` from the base button styles (line 385). This is the right fix — explicit color on the component that opts out of the default button background.

**Tests — `e2e/user-journey.spec.ts`**
- The visually-hidden nav test (lines 126-147) correctly asserts links are attached to the DOM but clipped to 1x1 pixel via `boundingBox()`. Good approach.
- The toggle visibility test (lines 149-177) correctly registers a user, adds a todo, then asserts the toggle button's computed color is not white. This is a solid regression guard.
- The full user journey test (lines 58-124) was properly updated to scope the "Create account" click to `.landing-hero` (line 64) so it clicks the visible CTA rather than the now-hidden nav link. This is important — without this change the test would have been clicking an invisible element.

**Integration tests**: The existing Rust integration tests in `tests/api/index.rs` still pass and remain adequate — they verify the HTML structure and link presence at the HTTP response level. The visual rendering behavior is correctly delegated to Playwright.

No concerns. I approve this change.

content.text:
Review sent. I approve both fixes. The changes are minimal, targeted, and correct:

1. **Template fix** (`templates/index.html`): `visually-hidden` class on nav links -- clean, no Rust changes needed.
2. **CSS fix** (`static/css/main.css`): `color: var(--color-fg)` on `.todo-item__toggle` -- properly overrides inherited white.
3. **Playwright tests**: Both regression tests are well-structured, and the existing full journey test was correctly updated to click the visible hero CTA instead of the now-hidden nav link.

The existing Rust integration tests remain adequate for HTTP-level validation; visual rendering is properly delegated to the e2e layer.
